featuredProject.title: Main Case
featuredProject.projectName: Khaos Kontrol - MicroSaaS for Beauty Professionals
featuredProject.description: Self-employed beauty professionals lose 8 to 12 hours a week juggling Calendly, spreadsheets and WhatsApp. Generic tools don't speak the niche's vocabulary; custom spreadsheets don't scale. Khaos Kontrol is the specialized SaaS: real-time scheduling, PDF contracts via edge function, Pix payments with webhook tracking and an operational AI assistant — all inside an Industrial Noir Design System spanning 60+ production screens.

items.quote: We needed to launch an MVP in 8 weeks and still integrate with AI. Paula designed in Figma, implemented in React + Supabase, and plugged in the AI edge function — all in 7.5 weeks. It's now the foundation of the paid product.
items.author: André Ferreira

website.challenge: The client needed a digital presence that did more than exist — it had to convert. Visitors arrive at an emotionally delicate moment, usually looking for family counseling. Slow pages, long forms or cold copy broke trust in the first seconds. The challenge was to pair measurable performance (LCP, CLS, SEO) with a welcoming journey that reduced friction before booking.
website.solution: I mapped the entire journey in Figma focused on two critical moments: the landing (where trust starts) and the booking (where trust turns into action). I implemented everything from scratch in React + TypeScript, with isolated hooks for lead capture and integration with the scheduling system. The hero is static-rendered for low LCP; everything else loads progressively. Typography and palette were chosen to convey credibility — Playfair Display for headings, medium weight CTAs.

process.title: Discovery
process.desc: Interviews with the client about audience profile, analysis of 6 direct competitors, and mapping of the emotional journey (vulnerability → trust → action).

process.title: Wireframing
process.desc: Low-fidelity flows tested with 3 users before final design. The key insight: cutting the booking form from 8 to 3 fields removed the biggest drop-off point.

process.title: Implementation
process.desc: Vite + React + TypeScript setup, accessible components (WCAG AA), integration with the scheduling API, and lead capture via a Zod-typed form.

process.title: Performance & SEO
process.desc: Schema.org markup, WebP images, route-level code-splitting, and semantic meta tags. Lighthouse > 95 on mobile.

results.description: in the first quarter after launch, compared to the previous site
Largest Contentful Paint measured on 4G, target was < 2.5s

portfolio.challenge: A portfolio claiming 'frontend engineer' has to prove engineering, not just show pictures. I wanted every interaction on the site to act as evidence: 60fps animations, strict TypeScript, real i18n in 3 languages, GSAP cinematic scroll, route-level code-splitting, and all of that with an initial bundle < 150 KB gzip. The risk was over-engineering for impressions and losing usability.
portfolio.solution: I built each piece thinking 'this would pass code review'. GSAP hero with pinned scroll trigger, page transitions with AnimatePresence, smooth scroll via Lenis synced with ScrollTrigger, dark mode with HSL tokens, form with reCAPTCHA v3 + Zod validation, first-party analytics through a Supabase Edge Function. Global ErrorBoundary, real-time Web Vitals reporting, hover-prefetch of routes. 'Industrial Noir' aesthetic: black, silver, Playfair + Inter, grain texture.

process.title: Visual direction
process.desc: 'Industrial Noir' moodboard inspired by editorial design — warm blacks, silver accents, serif paired with geometric sans.

process.title: Technical architecture
process.desc: Vite + React + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn/ui. Manual chunks vendor / motion / i18n / supabase / monitoring to control what makes it into first paint.

process.title: Motion design
process.desc: GSAP pinned hero zoom, ScrollSection wrapper with pin + scaleDown + parallax, Lenis with lagSmoothing, PageTransition with vertical slide reveal.

process.title: i18n & a11y
process.desc: 3 languages (PT/EN/ES), skip-to-content link, focus rings on every interactive, prefers-reduced-motion honored by GSAP.

results.description: at first paint — admin and case studies stay outside via React.lazy
PT, EN and ES with preference persisted in localStorage
no `any`, Zod schemas validating every I/O boundary

microsaas.challenge: Niche online courses see dropout rates above 70%. Off-the-shelf LMSs treat content like PDF folders — no rhythm, no progression, no pride. For this cultural-education project, I needed to turn passive consumption into a gamified journey, without becoming a childish 'edu-game'. The balance: visual rituals that respect the subject matter, with feedback loops that retain the student between modules.
microsaas.solution: LMS built from scratch. Level progression tied to real content milestones (not minutes watched), achievements with their own thematic identity, interactive quizzes over Supabase Realtime, and adaptive streaming. Every module closes with an 'elevation screen' — not just 'congrats, next' — reinforcing why it matters. The architecture is serverless: Supabase Auth + Postgres + Storage + Edge Functions; the frontend is React/TypeScript with a state machine for long flows.

process.title: Qualitative research
process.desc: Interviews with 12 students from similar courses to understand exactly when they drop. The pattern: module 3 of 8, with no visible progress feedback.

process.title: Progression system
process.desc: I designed the level / milestone system in Figma with high-fi prototypes. The rule: progression must be visible in < 1 second on screen and narratively tied to the content.

process.title: Functional MVP
process.desc: Supabase Auth, chunked content with video.js, real-time quizzes, XP / achievements via Postgres triggers.

process.title: Beta validation
process.desc: Closed cohort of 18 students. Weekly retention, per-module completion, and post-course interviews.

results.description: beta cohort average — industry benchmark is 2-3/8
Supabase channels syncing state across students
100% serverless — scales to any load without provisioning

dashboard.challenge: Founders with an idea skip validation and jump straight to Figma — or worse, straight to code. The result: 3 months later, an MVP is running and nobody uses it. The validation tools out there are either blank spreadsheets asking 'fill it in', or academic 40-question frameworks no one finishes. What was missing was a short, guided, honest path: from real problem to whether it's worth building.
dashboard.solution: ProductKlini is a microsaas that takes a raw idea and returns a structured diagnosis in under 30 minutes. Proprietary 4-step framework (problem → persona → value proposition → validation metric), with direct copy that cuts business-school jargon. No signup to explore, account created only when you want to save the project. Lean stack — React + Vite + Tailwind — continuous deploy on Lovable. Generative AI plays copilot: suggests persona cuts, generates value-prop variations, surfaces hypotheses to test first.

process.title: Desk research + 6 founder interviews
process.desc: Talked to founders in the idea-to-MVP stage to learn where they get stuck. Clear pattern: they skip validation because existing frameworks feel like homework, not business decisions.

process.title: 4-step framework + copy rewrite
process.desc: Cut dozens of templates (Lean Canvas, JTBD, Mom Test) down to essentials: 4 questions that actually change the build-or-not decision. Copy rewritten to sound like conversation, not a lesson.

process.title: UI in Figma + tokens in code
process.desc: Minimal Design System in Figma (Variables + Auto Layout) translated directly into Tailwind tokens. No hand-off: the approved prototype became the product.

process.title: Build & continuous deploy on Lovable
process.desc: React + Vite on Lovable for deploy-per-commit. GenAI integration to produce value-prop variations and surface test hypotheses from what the user wrote.

results.description: Average time to complete the full framework
Lean cut based on what actually changes a decision
Every copy or flow iteration ships in minutes

platform.challenge: Self-employed beauty professionals lose 8 to 12 hours per week on manual management: WhatsApp scheduling, paper contracts, untracked Pix payments, spreadsheets no one updates. Market solutions are either generic tools (Calendly, Trello) that don't speak the niche's language, or custom spreadsheets that scale poorly. The opportunity: a specialized SaaS, with a luxury brand visual identity and a serverless architecture that scales without changing stacks.
platform.solution: I built KONTROL as a finished product — not an MVP. 'Industrial Noir' Design System applied consistently across 60+ screens, real-time drag-and-drop calendar (Supabase Realtime channels), server-side PDF contract generation through edge functions, Pix payment flow with webhook tracking, and an AI assistant for operational questions. The frontend is React/Vite with a state machine for transactional flows; the backend is 100% Supabase (Postgres + Auth + Storage + Edge Functions).

process.title: Real-customer validation
process.desc: I worked directly with a pilot salon for a month, mapping every hour spent on management. Every minute saved became a functional requirement.

process.title: Industrial Noir Design System
process.desc: Color tokens, typography (Inter + Playfair Display + JetBrains Mono for data), spacing grid, motion language with Framer Motion. Internal docs for every component.

process.title: Core implementation
process.desc: Auth + RLS row-level security, real-time calendar, PDF contracts via edge function (pdf-lib), Pix payment with automatic reconciliation, typed financial dashboards.

process.title: Launch & iteration
process.desc: Gradual roll-out, observability via Sentry, custom metrics on Supabase analytics, weekly release cycle with beta clients.

results.description: per user, according to pilot cohort feedback
standardized with HSL tokens and motion language
from mockup to paying MVR, without outsourcing
